title: scanner.rs: witnesses map grows unboundedly during long scans when spend detection is slow
description:
In scanner.rs, spent note pruning happens once per block in scan_block() via spent_note_ids_in(). However:

1. spent_note_ids_in() only checks notes that are already in self.witnesses (the in-memory map).
2. The DB is queried for spent status, but only notes that payment.rs has called mark_notes_spent() on will be pruned.
3. If the scanner runs for many blocks without any payments (normal wallet usage), the witnesses map grows by one entry per received note, forever.

More critically: the witnesses are serialized and written to the DB on *every block* for *every note* in the map. With N unspent notes, each block scan does O(N) serializations and O(N) DB writes to the witnesses table. For a heavily-used wallet that receives many notes and spends them slowly, this creates O(notes * blocks) I/O.

Example: 100 unspent notes × 1000 blocks = 100,000 witness rows written per 1000 blocks. Each write is a separate SQL statement within save_block_state.

The witnesses map also holds strong memory references to IncrementalWitness structs, which are non-trivial in size. A wallet that has received many small notes (dust attack scenario) could exhaust memory.

The pruning after spent_note_ids_in() only removes notes that were spent AND are in the witnesses map. Notes added to the map during rescan that were then spent are correctly pruned, but the I/O bound is still O(unspent_notes × blocks_since_receive) for long-lived notes.
